reasoning: The document exhibits two major defects: a header injected mid-sentence, interrupting the reading order, and one bare URL not properly converted into a hyperlink. Since there are exactly 2 major defects (between 1 and 5) and no critical defects, count-based routing mandates HUMAN_REVIEW.

concerns.analysis: There is a header leakage issue that interrupts the reading flow mid-sentence. At the bottom of page 1, a sentence begins ('All comments received... considered in'). In the output, the running header 'FACT SHEET' from the top of page 2 is injected immediately after this, before the sentence concludes ('the final permit decision.'). This breaks a continuous sentence.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: ...will be considered in [page break] the final permit decision.
concerns.output_quote: All comments received during the public comment period or at the public hearings will be considered in [...] { "type": "heading", "level": 1, "text": "FACT SHEET" } [...] the final permit decision.
concerns.location: End of Page 1 / Start of Page 2

concerns.description: 1 bare URL(s) in text not converted to hyperlinks: https://deq.nc.gov/about/divisions/waste-management/laserfiche.
concerns.analysis: bare_url_count=1. URLs appear as plain text instead of clickable links. Per NCDIT criteria, link text not wrapped in href is a major defect.

concerns.description: Page number artifacts retained: Page 1 of 2

Header/footer elements retained in output (1): Page 1 of 2

concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=1. Header/footer elements are present in extraction data. This is common in multi-page government PDFs and is typically cosmetic — the renderer strips them from HTML output.

action_items: Fix the reading order issue where the header interrupts the sentence bridging pages 1 and 2.
Convert the bare URL into a proper hyperlink.
